name: How do I set up FAQ pages so that Google and voice assistants actually use my answers as featured snippets?
description: Turn each FAQ into a heading plus a concise answer paragraph, then add FAQPage schema so search engines understand the Q&A pairs. Use natural, spoken-style word

mainEntity.name: How do I set up FAQ pages so that Google and voice assistants actually use my answers as featured snippets?

acceptedAnswer.text:
To get your FAQs read aloud in voice search, you need both **solid on-page Q&A** and the right schema to tell assistants where the answers live.

Simply having an FAQ page isn’t enough if it’s not structured clearly.

**Key factors:**
- Make each question a heading and each answer a short, focused paragraph
- Add FAQPage schema so search engines see the Q&A relationships
- Use natural, conversational wording that matches how people speak
- Keep at least one question and answer visible on the page (not hidden behind tabs)

Review your current FAQ pages and convert any long-winded answers into concise primary responses plus optional detail. Validate the schema and monitor which questions start to gain impressions.

Well-structured FAQs can become your primary source of voice-search featured snippets.
acceptedAnswer.description: Turn each FAQ into a heading plus a concise answer paragraph, then add FAQPage schema so search engines understand the Q&A pairs. Use natural, spoken-style wording and keep at least one question–answer visible on the page. This structure makes your FAQs strong candidates for voice-search featured snippets.
